name: Korean TOPIK 2 Vocabulary
description: Intermediate Korean. TOPIK 2 vocabulary. Complex grammar, abstract topics, near-fluency.

facts.explanation: 어린아이 — young boy or girl. Part of speech: noun.
언어 — "The means of communication, such as sounds, letters, etc. for expressing or conveying one's thoughts or feelings; or the system of such means. ". Part of speech: noun.

얻다 — To take and have something without special efforts or without paying for it. Part of speech: verb. Also: To come to have or enjoy a positive attitude, response, state, etc, To seek or look for something and then have it, To get a loan, To lease a house, room, etc, To secure or get a right, result, property, etc, To come to find and use a worker.', "To take someone into one's family.", 'To become ill

얼다 — For a liquid or moist material to become solidified by a cold energy. Part of speech: verb. Also: For a part or all of the body to become extremely cold and numb because of chilly conditions, To be overly nervous because one is intimidated by the mood or the presence of someone else
엉덩이 — The fleshy part between the waist and thigh that touches the floor when one is sitting. Part of speech: noun.
여성 — A grown-up woman capable of having a baby. Part of speech: noun. Also: One of the terms used to distinguish the gender of words in the grammar of Western languages
여행지 — place where one travels. Part of speech: noun.
역시 — Likewise. Part of speech: adverb. Also: As one thought, As usual, No matter how one thinks
연결 — Two or more things or phenomena being connected or related to each other. Part of speech: noun.
연극 — An act in which an actor or actress acts on a stage according to a script for the audience. Part of speech: noun. Also: A remark or act designed to make others believe something fake as real
연락 — act of delivering a fact. Part of speech: noun.
연말 — "The year's end.". Part of speech: noun.
영수증 — A piece of paper on which the details of transaction of money or goods are written. Part of speech: noun.
옆집 — "A house next to one's house.". Part of speech: noun.
예매 — The act of buying a passenger ticket, admission ticket, etc., in advance. Part of speech: noun.

예술 — Arts and sciences. Part of speech: noun. Also: The activity of creating and expressing beauty by using particular materials, techniques, style, etc.; a piece of work produced in such a way, (figurative) Very beautiful and excellent skills
예습 — The act of studying in advance for the next class. Part of speech: noun.
오래되다 — Marked by a long duration of time since the start or formation of something. Part of speech: adjective.
오른손 — hand on the right side. Part of speech: noun.
온도 — The degree of warmth and cold; a numerical value indicating such a degree. Part of speech: noun.

올리다 — To make a price, figure, energy, etc., get higher or increase. Part of speech: verb. Also: To make a performance, achievement, etc., reach a high level, To make something move from a lower to a higher position, To hold a ceremony or ritual, To hold a service or ceremony or offer a prayer, To shout or shriek loudly, To give a slap on the cheek, etc, To make something reach a certain level, To make something laid on something else, To put food on a table, To have something as the topic of a conversation, To register; to fill in, To put tiles, etc., on a roof to complete the roof, To construct a building, To upload a file, post, article, etc., on a computer network or Internet newspaper.', "To greet, make a bow or speak to one's superior, or hand something to him/her.", "To present a document, etc., to one's superior or a superior organization."

옳다 — Being in accord with a rule and just. Part of speech: adjective. Also: Better than something, Right, not wrong.', "A word used to indicate one's feeling when something is exactly the same as one's thought.", 'A word used to indicate that one agrees to a certain opinion or idea.', "A word used to indicate one's feeling when something forgotten comes to one's memory suddenly."

가리키다 — To point a finger or some other thing in a certain direction or toward a certain object to let others recognize it. Part of speech: verb. Also: For hands of a clock or the sensor of a thermometer to indicate time or temperature, To convey a fact or meaning, To make reference to or mention something specifically
완전히 — In a manner of everything being provided without anything left out. Part of speech: adverb.

왕 — A king who is a ruler of a country where the crown is passed down from one to another member of the royal family. Part of speech: noun. Also: (figurative) The most outstanding figure in terms of power or value, etc., within a certain field or range
외우다 — To not forget but to remember a speech, composition, etc. Part of speech: verb. Also: To remember a speech, composition, etc., and say it perfectly
요리사 — person who cooks as an occupation. Part of speech: noun.
울음 — The act of making a sound and shedding tears at the same time. Part of speech: noun.
웃음 — The act of laughing or smiling, the way one laughs or smiles, or the sound that one makes when he/she laughs. Part of speech: noun.
유명 — "The state of one's name being widely known.". Part of speech: noun.
유치원 — The institute and facilities that are made to educate children before they start school. Part of speech: noun.
윷놀이 — playing yut; game of yut: A game in which players compete with pieces to reach the finish line first, as they throw a set of four yut sticks and move the pieces. Part of speech: noun.
음료 — Water or all kinds of liquid that one can drink like water. Part of speech: noun.
음식점 — business cooking and selling food. Part of speech: noun.

이기다 — "To beat and perform better than one's opponent in a bet, match, fight, etc.", 'To suppress a feeling, desire, etc. Part of speech: verb. Also: To stand and endure difficulties.', "To keep one's body upright or balanced."
이때 — Just now; the time mentioned in the very previous moment. Part of speech: noun.
이마 — The part of the face from the hairline to the eyebrows. Part of speech: noun. Also: The topmost part of an object that serves as its front
이미 — In a state in which something was done before the present. Part of speech: adverb.
이불 — "Something made of fabric, etc., to cover a person's body when sleeping.". Part of speech: noun.
이삿짐 — Goods that should be carried when one moves into a new house. Part of speech: noun.
이웃 — The state of being side by side with or close to a region or country.', "A house that is close to one's own or those who live in the house.". Part of speech: noun.
이전 — time preceding the present time. Part of speech: noun. Also: The time before and including a certain point of time
이후 — time after the present time. Part of speech: noun. Also: The time including and after a certain point of time

익숙하다 — Not bad at doing something because one did it several times before. Part of speech: adjective. Also: Not unfamiliar but comfortable with someone or something because one has seen him/her or experienced it often
공장 — A place where products are manufactured by processing raw materials and other materials. Part of speech: noun.
일부 — One portion of something, or part of the whole. Part of speech: noun.
입술 — The red and soft flesh that surrounds the opening of the mouth. Part of speech: noun.
입장권 — The ticket whose holder is allowed to enter the place of an event or performance. Part of speech: noun.

잎 — A vegetative organ of a plant, which is green, flat, and attached to the point or sides of a stem. Part of speech: noun. Also: A unit for counting the number of leaves of a tree, grass leaves, petals, etc, A word that is attached behind the name of a plant to indicate the leaf of the plant
자꾸 — Several times. Part of speech: adverb.

자라다 — For a living thing to get bigger partially or wholly. Part of speech: verb. Also: To grow up in a certain environment or background.', "For one's power, ability, etc., to increase.", 'To develop into something of a high level or state
자랑 — Saying things about oneself or people or things related to oneself in order to show off. Part of speech: noun.
자세히 — Concretely and clearly even in insignificant parts. Part of speech: adverb.
자식 — son and a daugther, a son or daughter. Part of speech: noun. Also: (endearing) A small child
자판기 — A machine that dispenses articles automatically when the customer inserts a coin or paper money into it and chooses an article. Part of speech: noun.
잡채 — A Korean dish made by mixing sweet potato noodles with thin slices of stir-fried various vegetables, meat, etc. Part of speech: noun.
장난감 — Various objects which children play with. Part of speech: noun.
저금 — The act of saving money or entrusting it to a financial institution like a bank, etc., or such money. Part of speech: noun.
적당하다 — Meeting requirements or conditions, or being appropriate in terms of degree. Part of speech: adjective. Also: Tactful enough to avoid causing a problem

전기 — An energy used to produce light or heat or to operate a machine. Part of speech: noun. Also: (figurative) The state of a body part being numb due to insufficient blood circulation, or a sharp sensation felt when one bumps into something
전부 — entire sum of all parts. Part of speech: noun.
전철 — A long, electrically-powered vehicle that runs on tracks and is capable of carrying a large number of passengers. Part of speech: noun.
전체 — Something considered as an entirety, which is composed of elements or parts. Part of speech: noun.
관광 — The act of going to a place to see or observe its scenery, environment, customs, etc. Part of speech: noun.
점점 — Little by little as times goes by. Part of speech: adverb.

접다 — To bend a piece of cloth, paper, etc., so that one part covers another. Part of speech: verb. Also: To bend a piece of paper so that one part covers another to form a shape, To return an unfolded item to its original shape.', "To stop thinking about something or give up one's opinion.", 'To give advantages to a lesser-skilled opponent
관광객 — person who goes on a tour. Part of speech: noun.
관광지 — A place worthy of traveling to due to its great scenery, historical sites, hot springs, etc. Part of speech: noun.

facts.correctAnswer: to get wet, to get soaked
facts.explanation: 젖다 — To become wet by absorbing liquid. Part of speech: verb. Also: To become accustomed after being repeatedly influenced by something, To be engrossed in certain emotions or thoughts, To become familiar with something after listening to it frequently, (figurative) For the sky to turn into a certain hue, For tears to well up in the eyes, For a voice to sound sad or touched
조심 — The act of being careful in speech, behavior, etc., not to get in trouble. Part of speech: noun.
졸다 — To enter a state where one has not fallen asleep but gets close to falling asleep repeatedly. Part of speech: verb.

줄이다 — To make the length, area, volume, etc., of an object smaller than original. Part of speech: verb. Also: To make a number or quantity smaller than original, To keep a state from reaching its original intensity, At the end of a speech or writing, to stop and finish although one has a lot more to say

줍다 — To pick up something that has dropped or dispersed on the ground. Part of speech: verb. Also: To pick up an item that someone has lost.', "To bring an abandoned child into one's household in order to raise him/her.", "To take or bring anything that one can get one's hands on."

중간 — space between two objects. Part of speech: noun. Also: The middle one among a set of grades, sizes, sequences, etc, middle of a space or time, A time during which something is going on, Something that exists between two extremes, middle-person who connects people

중앙 — The very center of a certain place or object. Part of speech: noun. Also: An important place that serves as the center, The city that is home to the government and serves as the center of the nation
교사 — A person with teaching qualifications who teaches students in a kindergarten, elementary school, middle school, high school, etc. Part of speech: noun.
지난해 — previous year. Part of speech: noun.
지루하다 — Feeling dull and tiresome because a certain act or state continues. Part of speech: adjective.
지우다 — To remove something written or drawn, or a trace, etc., using a tool.', "To remove or get rid of one's thought or memory.", "To remove one's emotion, expression, etc.". Part of speech: verb.
진짜 — Something that is neither an imitation nor fake. Part of speech: noun.

facts.correctAnswer: to be thick, to be dark
facts.explanation: 진하다 — concentration of a liquid being high. Part of speech: adjective. Also: density of a gas being high, Thick in color, Having a strong taste or smell, emotion being stronger than usual, The level of something being stronger or more intense
집들이 — "An event in which one invites one's close friends to a new house and serves food after moving.". Part of speech: noun.
집안일 — Work done in a household, such as the laundry, cleaning, cooking, etc. Part of speech: noun. Also: Work or events related to the household such as ancestral rites, weddings, etc
교육 — "The act of teaching knowledge, culture, techniques, etc., in order to enhance one's ability.". Part of speech: noun.
짜증 — "An act of expressing one's anger or dislike towards something because one is dissatisfied, or such a disposition.". Part of speech: noun.

짝 — Either one or both of two things that form a pair or set. Part of speech: noun. Also: A unit of counting pieces that form a pair or set, (slang) A partner, The word that indicates an incomparably extreme degree
찌다 — "To put more flesh on one's body and become fat.". Part of speech: verb.
찬물 — Cold water. Part of speech: noun.

참다 — To suppress and endure physiological phenomena such as laughter, crying, pain, coughing, etc. Part of speech: verb. Also: To suppress and control emotions such as sadness, fear, anger, etc, To endure hardships and difficulties, To endure and wait for a certain time
첫날 — The day when something starts for the first time. Part of speech: noun.
청년 — A young person who is mature both physically and mentally. Part of speech: noun.
청소년 — A person in his/her teens who is not yet an adult. Part of speech: noun.
체육관 — building designed for indoor exercise. Part of speech: noun.
쳐다보다 — To look upwards from below.', "To lift one's face and look at something or someone at eye level.", 'To rely on something or someone entirely. Part of speech: verb.
교환 — An act of changing something for something else. Part of speech: noun. Also: act of giving and receiving
초대장 — A written request to be present in a certain place, gathering, event, etc. Part of speech: noun.
최근 — The period from a past date, not long ago, to the present date. Part of speech: noun.
출석 — An act of going to a class, meeting, etc., and being there. Part of speech: noun.
출입 — An act of someone going in and out of a certain place. Part of speech: noun.
출입국 — act of leaving or entering a country. Part of speech: noun.
출퇴근 — act of going to work or leaving work. Part of speech: noun.
충분하다 — Being ample, without shortage. Part of speech: adjective.
침실 — A room in one’s home, where one usually sleeps. Part of speech: noun.
칭찬 — An act of expressing one’s appreciation of someone’s strength or achievement, etc., or such a remark. Part of speech: noun.
칼국수 — Noodles made by flattening the dough as thin as possible and cutting it into long strips with a knife; or a dish made by boiling them with other ingredients in a broth. Part of speech: noun.
코끼리 — The largest animal living on land that has a very long nose and two large tusks. Part of speech: noun.
콘서트 — A gathering organized so that people can listen to songs or music being sung or played live. Part of speech: noun.

큰소리 — A loud voice when scolding someone.', "One's words spoken confidently as if one were smarter, or one's words exaggerating things.", 'Words spoken confidently to someone, instead of obeying him/her. Part of speech: noun.

키우다 — To take care of an animal or plant so that it grows. Part of speech: verb. Also: To increase the length, area, volume, etc., of something, To teach someone to become a person with a special ability, To make a certain energy or power become bigger
태도 — "One's manner of moving one's body or dealing with a certain incident.", "One's position or thought about a certain incident.". Part of speech: noun.
택배 — An act of delivering a mail, package, goods, etc., to a requested destination, in person. Part of speech: noun.
토끼 — An animal with long ears and a short tail whose hind legs are more developed than its front legs. Part of speech: noun.
통화 — An act of talking with someone using a telephone. Part of speech: noun. Also: unit for counting phone calls
튀기다 — To put an ingredient in boiling oil, so that it inflates. Part of speech: verb. Also: To heat dried grains, etc., making them inflate
트럭 — vehicle that carries cargo. Part of speech: noun.
특별하다 — Being different from what is normal, with a noticeable gap. Part of speech: adjective.

튼튼하다 — Very hard and firm.', "One's body, bones, teeth, etc., being hard, firm, and healthy.", 'A group or an organization being stable and not easily collapsed,', "One's ideology or mind not shaking and being certain.". Part of speech: adjective.
편리 — The state of something being easy and comfortable to use. Part of speech: noun.
편의점 — A store that is open 24 hours a day and sells daily necessities, etc. Part of speech: noun.

굵다 — An object being long in circumference or wide in width. Part of speech: adjective. Also: A chestnut, jujube, egg, etc., being larger than the average, A water drop, rain drop, etc., being large in volume, The strokes of letters being clearer and bigger, The scope of thinking, behavior, etc., being wide and broad, The reverberation of a sound being strong and at low decibels, A fabric being coarse and rough because it has been made of thick threads, Loosely woven and having wide holes
평소 — Ordinary times in which nothing special is happening. Part of speech: noun.

푸르다 — Clear and vivid blue, like that of a clear fall sky, deep ocean, or fresh grass. Part of speech: adjective. Also: Grains, fruits, etc., being in an unripe state, power being strong, (figurative) Being full of youth and livelihood, (figurative) A hope, dream, etc., being big and beautiful, Air, etc., being clear and fresh, Feeling chilly and frigid
풍경 — beautiful scene of nature or an area. Part of speech: noun. Also: A special scene or situation that evokes an emotion
프라이팬 — A low, wide pan with a handle, used to stir-fry or fry food in oil. Part of speech: noun.
궁금하다 — Having a strong desire to know about something. Part of speech: adjective. Also: Feeling heavyhearted worrying about something
한가하다 — Having time to spare without haste. Part of speech: adjective.
한옥 — A traditional Korean house or a house built in such a style. Part of speech: noun.
귀걸이 — Accessories for the ears. Part of speech: noun.
항공 — act of flying in the air on a plane. Part of speech: noun.
항공권 — A ticket whose holder is allowed to board a plane. Part of speech: noun.
해마다 — Each and every year. Part of speech: adverb.
행동 — "An act of moving one's body to do a certain task or action. ". Part of speech: noun.

헤어지다 — To part with another one has been with.', "To cut off one's relationship with someone one is on close terms with.", 'For something stuck together to fall apart or scatter. Part of speech: verb. Also: For the skin to be split and cracked
헬스클럽 — "A place equipped with a variety of exercise apparatuses or facilities to improve one's health or keep one's body in good shape. ". Part of speech: noun.
호수 — large pond surrounded by land. Part of speech: noun.

혹시 — Not probably but possibly. Part of speech: adverb. Also: By chance, Perhaps as one has guessed, The word that is used when one is hesitant to say something he/she thinks is possible but he/she is uncertain about
귀찮다 — Disliking and feeling tired of doing something. Part of speech: adjective.

화려하다 — Looking good, being beautiful, bright, and shiny.', "One's job or life being too luxurious and fancy to be enjoyed by average people.", 'A sentence coming with many modifiers.', "Having a lot of great experiences in one's career.". Part of speech: adjective.
환전 — "The act of changing one country's currency into another's. ". Part of speech: noun.
회원 — A person who is part of a certain meeting. Part of speech: noun.
훌륭하다 — Extremely good and excellent enough to be praised. Part of speech: adjective.
훨씬 — In the state of something being greatly different from something else it is being compared to. Part of speech: adverb.
휴게실 — room arranged for taking a brief rest. Part of speech: noun.
휴대폰 — A portable, wireless phone with which one can make or receive phone calls. Part of speech: noun.

흐르다 — For a liquid like water, etc., to move to a lower place or pass a certain location. Part of speech: verb. Also: For time or years to go by, To glide in the air or on the water, For gas, electricity, etc., to move along a cable or pipe, For blood, sweat, tears, etc., to come out and fall from the body, For powder or particles to leak or fall through a small hole, For light, sound, smell, etc., to spread gently, For a certain energy, state, etc., to show on the outside, For a certain mood, thought, etc., to continue in a certain way, For gloss, luster, etc., to show in a shiny manner, For a person or thing to take on a tendency or to move disproportionately in a direction

흔들다 — To make something move from side to side and back and forth, repeatedly. Part of speech: verb. Also: To make an extremely loud sound or shock echo through an object or across a space, To shock the current order or authority, or something in a state of peace, or cause it to make a big move.', "To move one's heart or weaken one's will.", 'To make something move as one pleases

흘리다 — To let water or tiny grains, etc., leak out or fall. Part of speech: verb. Also: To drop something carelessly in the wrong place, To make secret or information, etc., secretly known to another, To exhibit a certain feeling or expression briefly, To let sweat, tears, nasal discharge, blood, saliva, etc., out of the body, To miss or forget what other say without listening carefully to it, To write characters roughly, not neatly
희망 — The act of having expectations for the future. Part of speech: noun. Also: The potentional to be successful in the future
가꾸다 — To take care of plants so that they can grow well.', "To make one's appearance look nice or to maintain its condition.", 'To make something better. Part of speech: verb.
가난 — "One's state of having insufficient money or this state itself.". Part of speech: noun.
가능 — The state of being able to do or be something. Part of speech: noun.
가능성 — extent to which something can happen. Part of speech: noun. Also: The ability that can be developed in the future

가라앉다 — For something on the surface of or in the midst of water, etc., to go down to the bottom. Part of speech: verb. Also: For a bad weather to become warmer or for a strong natural phenomenon to become weaker, For a strong emotion or feeling to fade away slowly, For the symptom of a disease to get better, For a boisterous situation to become quiet, For a voice, facial expression, etc., to sink
가렵다 — "Feeling like to scratch one's skin.". Part of speech: adjective.

가리다 — To distinguish and single out one from many things. Part of speech: verb. Also: To be reluctant to meet strangers due to shyness, etc, To sort out or divide things according to a certain standard such as right or wrong, good or bad, etc, For children to be able to use the toilet.', "To be picky with food and therefore eat only one's favorite food.", "To take care of one's matters on one's own."
가만히 — Without movement or words. Part of speech: adverb. Also: Without any action or effort to solve something, With calm and care, Quietly so that others will not notice
그대로 — Exactly as the original shape or state. Part of speech: adverb. Also: In the same way as that
가뭄 — A weather condition with no rain for a long time. Part of speech: noun.
가사 — Words written to music. Part of speech: noun.
가스 — substance in a gaseous state. Part of speech: noun. Also: Gas used as fuel, toxic gas that can kill a person, Gas that occurs as food digests in the stomach
가습기 — A device making water vapor to adjust room humidity. Part of speech: noun.
가입 — The act of joining a group or signing up for goods and services. Part of speech: noun.
가전제품 — Electronic appliances used in the house. Part of speech: noun.
가정 — A community consisting of family members or the house they live in. Part of speech: noun.
가치 — Price or the degree of valuableness. Part of speech: noun. Also: Meaning or importance
각자 — "Each person or an individual's own self. ". Part of speech: noun.
각종 — Many different kinds of something. Part of speech: noun.
간격 — The distance between two spatial points, or the temporal distance between two points in time. Part of speech: noun. Also: The degree of how far apart two people or objects are
간접적 — The state of something being indirectly affected, not at the center of an event or situation. Part of speech: noun.

간판 — A signboard containing the name of a shop, institution, etc., which is hung or standing outside a building or attached to the façade of a building so as to be noticed by people. Part of speech: noun. Also: (figurative) Something one can show off as a trait that represents oneself.', "(slang) One's appearance, education, career, qualification, etc., that represents oneself."
간호 — An act of taking care of someone who is sick. Part of speech: noun.
갈다 — To put something away and fill its place with another. Part of speech: verb. Also: To take the place of someone
갈등 — A situation where two parties are at odds with each other.', "One's agony and inner struggle over what to decide.". Part of speech: noun.
갈수록 — More and more in degree over time. Part of speech: adverb.

감각 — "One's physical perception of a stimulus through one's eyes, ears, nose, tongue, and skin.", "One's ability to judge or express something based on what is felt and understood.". Part of speech: noun.

감독 — The act of monitoring and controlling things or people so that nothing goes wrong. Part of speech: noun. Also: A person who is responsible for and directs the entire process at a concert, in making a movie, preparing for and playing a sport, etc
감동적 — A moving of the heart or spirit due to strong feelings. Part of speech: noun.
감상 — The act of enjoying, understanding, and assessing a piece of art or scenery. Part of speech: noun.
감소 — Quantity or a number reduction. Part of speech: noun.
감정 — A feeling or mood one has toward a certain incident or person. Part of speech: noun.
강물 — Water running in a river. Part of speech: noun.
강사 — A person teaching students at schools or institutes of learning. Part of speech: noun.
강수량 — The quantity of water that comes from rain or snow that fall on a particular location during a particular period of time. Part of speech: noun.
강의 — An act of teaching knowledge or technology systematically at a university, cram school, or an institute. Part of speech: noun.
강의실 — classroom used for lectures. Part of speech: noun.
강조 — The act of making something stand out or insisting on something strongly. Part of speech: noun.

갖추다 — To make or possess something one is expected to have. Part of speech: verb. Also: To adopt an attitude or mindset that one is expected to have, To follow or comply with required procedures or formalities

개구리 — An animal with four webbed feet and long hind legs, good for swimming and leaping, respectively, which lives in rice paddies or streams, and makes a sound by inflating its vocal sac. Part of speech: noun.
개다 — For snow, rain, fog, etc., to let up or disappear, leading to a clear weather. Part of speech: verb.

개발 — The act of making land, natural resources, etc. useful, or easy to utilize.', "The act of improving one's ability, knowledge, etc.", 'The act of improving or expanding the economy of a nation or a certain industry. Part of speech: noun. Also: The act of creating a new product or idea

개방 — The act of opening the entrance to a place for people to enter or use freely. Part of speech: noun. Also: The act of removing a ban on something and letting people or goods travel or interact freely
그러므로 — An adverb used when the preceding statement is the reason for, grounds for, or condition of the following statement. Part of speech: adverb.
개선 — The act of reforming shortcomings, wrong or bad sides to character and making them better. Part of speech: noun.
개성 — A unique feature that distinguishes one thing from other things. Part of speech: noun.
개인적 — Something owned by an individual, or related to an individual. Part of speech: noun.
개최 — Organized planning and holding of gatherings, events, sports meets, etc. Part of speech: noun.
객관적 — Viewing or thinking a fact or thing as it is or as anyone would agree, without engaging personal views or emotions. Part of speech: noun.
거래 — The act of exchanging, selling or buying money or goods. Part of speech: noun. Also: The act of coming and going in order to maintain social relationships
